channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Analyst urges restraint as leaders politicise Utumishi Girls fire tragedy
item.description: Political analyst and communication strategist Advice Mundalo has criticised the growing tendency among Kenyan political actors to politicise national tragedies, following the Utumishi Girls Academy f...

item.title: Serengeti Boys Qualify for FIFA U17 World Cup, Wenger Applauds Youth Team
item.description: Legendary football coach Arsène Wenger has congratulated Tanzania’s Under-17 team, the Serengeti Boys, after they made history by qualifying for the FIFA U17 World Cup in Qatar. Wenger, who is now FIF...

item.title: Laikipia County Leaders Oppose Proposed Ebola Quarantine Facility Over Safety and Consultation Concerns
item.description: Laikipia county leaders have strongly opposed plans to set up a US-requested Ebola quarantine and treatment centre within the county. Governor Joshua Wakahora Irungu stated that the facility was propo...

item.title: Lessons from the Netherlands on reducing youth unemployment in the UK
item.description: The article examines how the Netherlands has achieved one of the lowest rates of young people not in education, employment or training (NEET) in Europe, and what lessons the United Kingdom could draw ...

item.title: Weekly roundup of humorous parenting posts from social media (May 23–29)
item.description: This HuffPost article features a weekly compilation of humorous posts from parents shared across social media platforms such as X (formerly Twitter), Threads, and Bluesky. Published as part of a recur...

item.title: PTI Leaders, Including Junaid Akbar, Allegedly Detained in Gilgit-Baltistan During Election Preparations
item.description: The Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) has alleged that its Khyber Pakhtunkhwa president, MNA Junaid Akbar, along with MNAs Saleemur Rehman, Amjab Ali Khan, Syed Mehboob Shah, MPA Naeem and Dr. Nawaz, wer...

item.title: Tony Abbott elected Liberal Party president signals push to rebuild and expand party base
item.description: Former Australian prime minister Tony Abbott has been elected unopposed as federal president of the Liberal Party, signalling a renewed push to reshape and rebuild the organisation ahead of the next f...

item.title: Canada's Economy Sees Slight Contraction in Early 2026, Technical Recession Noted
item.description: Canada's economy experienced a minor contraction in the first quarter of 2026, shrinking at an annualized rate of 0.1 per cent, according to Statistics Canada. This marks the second consecutive quarte...

item.title: FBI Interviews Wisconsin Election Officials About 2020 Presidential Election
item.description: Federal agents from the FBI's Milwaukee field office have been interviewing current and former election officials in Wisconsin as part of a broader review of the 2020 presidential election results in ...

item.title: CHED and Bam Aquino introduce P25,000 AHEAD grant to support nursing and allied health students
item.description: The Commission on Higher Education (CHED), in partnership with former Senator Bam Aquino, has launched a new financial assistance programme called the AHEAD Grant aimed at supporting students pursuing...

item.title: Families Struggle to Confirm Status of Students After Utumishi Girls Fire
item.description: The tragic fire at Utumishi Girls Academy left 16 students dead and many families in deep grief, with uncertainty still hanging over some children’s whereabouts. Parents and relatives visited the scho...

item.title: Understanding the different types of fitness: aerobic and anaerobic explained
item.description: Fitness is often thought of simply as being strong, lean or athletic, but exercise science shows it is far more complex. According to the article, fitness can be broken down into several components, i...

item.title: Canadian emergency departments increasingly treating patients in waiting rooms and non-clinical spaces amid hospital bed shortages
item.description: Canadian emergency departments are increasingly relying on what physicians describe as “chair care,” where patients are assessed and treated in emergency room waiting areas, hallways, closets, and oth...

item.title: Scientific and regulated approach to offshore energy development in South Africa
item.description: South Africa's offshore oil and gas sector is positioned at a critical point, with the potential to bolster domestic energy security and economic growth. Governed by the National Environmental Managem...

item.title: Police shelter-in-place order restricts access to street and school in Calgary’s Temple neighbourhood
item.description: A shelter-in-place order was issued in Calgary’s Temple neighbourhood, resulting in the closure of a street and the temporary restriction of access to a local school. The police directive affected res...

item.title: Jinggoy Estrada posts bail in graft case, avoids detention for now
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada remained free after posting bail in connection with a graft case linked to an alleged flood control corruption scandal, following proceedings at the Sandiganbayan on May 29, 20...

item.title: Guzman y Gomez exits US market after closing all Chicago-area restaurants
item.description: Guzman y Gomez, an Australian-based Mexican fast-food chain, has withdrawn from the United States after shutting down all of its Chicago-area restaurants. The company, which operates more than 260 loc...

item.title: GCash Fuse expands access to formal digital credit for Filipino MSMEs and underserved borrowers
item.description: The article highlights how GCash’s lending arm, Fuse Financing Inc., is helping more Filipinos and micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) access formal credit through digital lending services li...

item.title: Chicago U.S. Attorney Denies DOJ Investigation Into E. Jean Carroll Amid Reporting Confusion
item.description: The U.S. attorney for the Northern District of Illinois has firmly denied reports that the Department of Justice opened a criminal investigation into writer E. Jean Carroll. Andrew Boutros issued a st...

item.title: Government announces Rs22 per litre cut in petrol and diesel prices amid global oil trends
item.description: The government of Pakistan has announced a significant reduction in fuel prices, cutting both petrol and high-speed diesel (HSD) rates by Rs22 per litre each with immediate effect. This decision comes...
